core_mechanism: a discretization-convention-dependent drift correction that maps one interpretation of a noise-driven integral equation to another — i.e., the same stochastic integral evaluated under different limiting conventions yields dynamics that differ by a deterministic drift term, and one convention admits a decomposition into gradient flow on a potential landscape with a stationary Boltzmann-Gibbs (exponential-of-potential) measure
brief_reason: The abstract states that an SDE alone is underdetermined until a stochastic-integration interpretation is fixed, that different interpretations specify different dynamics, and it derives the concise (drift) relation linking a new interpretation — which carries a built-in Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution and potential function — to the Ito process.
